calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-15-2018 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar May-04-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
James Weixel, Esq. 415-949-1900 of Demler Armstrong & Rowland LLP for Defendant FARMERS INSURANCE GROUP, TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE, FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE.
No Other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT FARMERS INSURANCE GROUP, TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE, FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summary Adjudication.

Defendants Farmers Insurance Group, Truck Insurance Exchange, and Fire Insurance Exchange's motion for summary judgment is GRANTED. The court grants summary adjudication on the cause of action for breach of contract based on Truck's commercial insurance policy. Vasquez does not dispute that the policy included a cooperation provision. (Defendants' UMF 4; Parkhurst Dec., Ex. A, sec. I.E.3.a.) The undisputed evidence shows that on 2/7/13, defendants requested additional documents. (Parkhurst Dec., Ex. D.) Vasquez failed to respond to this letter and violated the cooperation provision as a matter of law. The requested information was material and defendants were prejudiced. "Where the insurer has reason to suspect arson, it is relevant and material to inquire into the financial condition of the insured'"(Abdelhamid v. Fire Ins. Exch. (2010) 182 Cal.App.4th 990, 1001.) The letter sought recent tax statements and Vasquez failed to provide them, which hampered the investigation. Vasquez cites his declaration to argue that he provided all documents that Francis orally requested. Defendants correctly note that Vasquez's declaration is inadmissible because it does not comply with CCP 2015.5 and the court sustains defendants' objection to that declaration. In addition, Vasquez's claim on the commercial policy is excluded under the dishonesty exclusion. (Parkhurst Dec., Ex. A sec. I.B.1.f.) Vasquez was running an illegal auto body shop. The court grants summary adjudication on the cause of action for breach of contract based on the Fire Insurance Exchange's homeowner's policy. The policy provides that Vasquez has one-year to file a claim. (Sanck Dec., Ex. A, Condition 11.) Vasquez does not dispute this fact. (Defendants' UMF 14.) The undisputed evidence demonstrates that Vasquez first made a claim on 11/18/15. (Sanck Dec., Ex. E.) Therefore, the claim is untimely. Vasquez argues that defendants are estopped from raising the time bar issue because a letter dated 2/11/13 refers to the statute of limitations expiring on 11/21/15. (Parkhurst Dec., Ex. E, pg. 3.) That letter related to the commercial policy, not the homeowners policy. Vasquez does not dispute that "Farmers Insurance Group" is not a legal entity. (Defendants' UMF 44.) Therefore, Farmers Insurance Group is not a proper party to this case and summary judgment is granted. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order and Judgment are signed. (see scanned order and judgment for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano , CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/RBU)
